reasoning: SPY at 710.90, near premarket high 710.92. Overnight gap of +$2.21 is intact and supported by a strong BULL ribbon stack (fast 710.27 > pivot 710.09 > slow 709.42, spread 86¢). The critical support zone is 709.46–709.91: if price holds above this, the battle level 712.39 (Carry tier, 5-day RTH high) becomes the primary target. If the premarket high (710.92) gives way, the next decision point is whether price can reclaim the prior session RTH high (709.91); failure there opens the bear case to 706.14. Current price location (sandwiched between PMH 710.92 overhead and PML 709.46 below, both only 1.4–2.4 handles away) creates asymmetric risk/reward: ~$1.50 to the battle level (712.39) versus ~$1.45 to the PML. The ribbon's bullish bias tilts conviction to the upside, contingent on the support hold.

primary.condition: if SPY holds 709.91 and breaks 710.92 PMH

secondary.condition: if SPY breaks below 709.46 PML (gap-floor loss)

key_observations: Premarket high (710.92) reached on first test; current price 710.90 sits at the resistance. Microstructure test: can buyers push through PMH on the open, or does it reject?
The overnight gap (+$2.21) is fully intact with support at PML 709.46 (1.44 handles below). This is a healthy gap structure; no sellers trapped above the open.
BULL ribbon stack with 86-cent spread signals confidence. The slow EMA (709.42) is well below current price, creating a cushion. First support on any pull-in is the pivot (710.09), then the PML.

Battle level 712.39 is a Carry-tier level (5-day RTH high), meaning it has cross-session weight. If the open doesn't gap-and-go above 710.92, intraday range is likely 709.46–712.39. The width (~$3) suggests a trending session if the buyers break overhead.
